title: Making Things Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A shape with four straight sides that are all the same length.
rounds.explanation: square: A shape with four straight sides that are all the same length.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A shiny grey colour, like a spoon or a coin.
rounds.explanation: silver: A shiny grey colour, like a spoon or a coin.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A man in stories who rules a whole country.
rounds.explanation: king: A man in stories who rules a whole country.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A round shape with no corners, like the sun.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A shiny yellow colour, like a special ring.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A brave person in stories who helps people in trouble.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Musa draws a square on his paper.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Aisha paints her paper star silver.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Musa has made a red square. Which sentence describes it?
rounds.explanation: The colour and the shape must both match, so only this sentence is true.

rounds.prompt: Which thing do you use to cut paper?
rounds.explanation: Scissors cut; glue sticks, a crayon colours and a ruler helps draw straight lines.

rounds.prompt: Which thing do you use to stick two pieces of paper together?
rounds.explanation: Glue sticks things; scissors cut them apart, a pencil writes and a rubber takes writing away.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What are Amal and Ayeeyo making under the big tree?
choices: She wove the mat slowly and carefully, without rushing
To show her that trying again makes work better
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: A mat, from soft grass

rounds.prompt: Ayeeyo said Amal was patient. What did Amal do slowly?
choices: She took a deep breath and tried again
She wove the mat slowly and carefully, without rushing
rounds.answer: She wove the mat slowly and carefully, without rushing
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She wove the mat slowly and carefully, without rushing

rounds.prompt: Why did Ishmael tell Amal about his own first picture?
choices: To show her that trying again makes work better
She took a deep breath and tried again
rounds.answer: To show her that trying again makes work better
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: To show her that trying again makes work better

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
rounds.target: An adult holds up a shape card and names it, then you name the next one.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
rounds.target: Tell your partner what you are cutting as you work.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Role-play
rounds.target: Play a dressing-up game and say what you have on.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Yusuf wears grey trousers to school.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The king wears a gold crown.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Aisha likes to paint big flowers.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'shirt' mean in this unit?
choices: Clothing for your top, with sleeves and buttons.
A shape with four straight sides that are all the same length.
A shape with four sides, two long and two short.
rounds.answer: Clothing for your top, with sleeves and buttons.
rounds.explanation: shirt: Clothing for your top, with sleeves and buttons.

rounds.prompt: What does 'cook' mean in this unit?
choices: A shape with four straight sides that are all the same length.
A shape with four sides, two long and two short.
A person who makes food for other people to eat.
rounds.answer: A person who makes food for other people to eat.
rounds.explanation: cook: A person who makes food for other people to eat.

rounds.prompt: What does 'rectangle' mean in this unit?
choices: The colour of fresh grass and new leaves.
A shape with four sides, two long and two short.
A shape with four straight sides that are all the same length.
rounds.answer: A shape with four sides, two long and two short.
rounds.explanation: rectangle: A shape with four sides, two long and two short.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'glasses'?
rounds.explanation: glasses is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'help'?
rounds.explanation: help is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'purple'?
rounds.explanation: purple is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A shape with three straight sides and three pointy corners.
The colour of fresh grass and new leaves.
The darkest colour, like the sky at night.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Something you wear on your head to keep it warm.
Clothing for your legs, with one part for each leg.
A funny person in a costume who makes people laugh.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: The person who flies an aeroplane through the sky.
To put colour on paper with a brush.
To tell someone you are happy for their kindness.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which shape is round with no corners?
rounds.explanation: A circle is round and has no corners; the other three shapes all have corners.

rounds.prompt: Which of these words tells you the colour of something?
rounds.explanation: Green is a colour; round tells a shape, paper is a thing and cut is an action.

rounds.prompt: Aisha mixes red paint with yellow paint. Which colour does she make?
rounds.explanation: Red and yellow together make orange; blue and yellow would make green.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word names a shape?
rounds.explanation: A circle is a shape; yellow is a colour, glue is a thing we use and soft tells how something feels.

rounds.prompt: Which shape has three sides?
rounds.explanation: A triangle has three sides; a circle has none and a square and rectangle have four.

rounds.prompt: Which shape has four sides that are all the same length?
rounds.explanation: All four sides of a square are equal; a triangle has three sides, a circle is round and a line is straight.
